hypothesis: Incorporating the Force Delta as a differentiable regularization term during training of direct-force NNIPs (i.e., adding λ·UΔ(R)^2 to the training loss, with λ selected on a validation set) will produce models whose RMS force error on adversarially generated out-of-distribution structures is at least 20% lower and whose Spearman rank correlation between UΔ and εdirect on those OOD structures increases by at least 0.05 relative to otherwise-identical models trained without the regularizer.
